problem_statement: AI coding assistants increase code production velocity, expanding the volume of code requiring review and turning code review into a growing bottleneck, while current AI review support is fragmented across isolated tasks rather than the end-to-end PR workflow.
magnitude_evidence: AI coding assistants accelerate individual coding tasks by more than 50% [13], but coordination time for integration grows faster than individual output and AI-generated contributions require more review iterations than human-written ones
insufficiency_evidence: Kononenko et al. reported that 54% of code reviews on the Mozilla project failed to identify bugs present in approved commits [89]; Czerwonka et al. found only 15% of reviewer comments pointed to potential defects; and when AI assists review reviewers surface more low-severity issues but not additional high-severity defects [16]
solution_mechanisms: specialized LLM agents with human-controlled quality gates
semantic diff map with provenance anchors
key_constructs: AI-powered code review framework (five coordinated stages)
notes: Vision/position paper; full text truncated mid-Section 4.5 (reference list retained). Draws heavily on secondary literature for problem quantification.
